eventCreationFlow: There is NO POST /api/events and NO explicit event-creation UI. Events are created exclusively as a side effect of chat. Flow: (1) Sidebar "+" (EventSidebar.handleNewEvent) just does setActiveEvent(null) + clearMessages() — pure client state reset, nothing hits the server. (2) User submits a message in ChatPanel (or WhiteboardInput); client POSTs /api/chat with body {message: string, eventId: activeEventId} where eventId is null for a new event. (3) Server (src/app/api/chat/route.ts -> orchestrate() in src/agents/orchestrator/index.ts): checks credits; if eventId null or getEvent() misses, calls createEvent(userId=session.user.email, {name: extractEventName(message)}) (src/lib/db/events.ts) -> DynamoDB item {id: uuid(), userId, name, type:'', date:'', attendeeCount:0, budget:{total:0,currency:'SGD',categories:[]}, preferences:{}, status:'draft', createdAt, updatedAt} with PK EVENT#<id>/SK METADATA and 90-day TTL. If name was 'New Event' and history empty it re-extracts a name and sets status:'planning'. (4) User message persisted via createMessage(eventId,'user',message); 2 base credits deducted; runAgentGraph(message, last-10-message history) runs; +1 credit per tool used; persistEventDetails() regex-extracts details from the response; assistant message persisted with metadata {agentName:'Eventiq', creditsCost, toolsUsed}. (5) Response JSON includes eventId. (6) Client: `if (data.eventId && !activeEventId) { useAppStore.getState().setActiveEvent(data.eventId); fetchEvents(); }` — this adoption step is CRITICAL; skipping it makes every subsequent message create a new duplicate event. The sidebar list comes from GET /api/events (summaries sorted pinned-first then lastActivity desc).
chatProtocol: POST /api/chat (src/app/api/chat/route.ts). NOT streaming — plain request/response JSON, single await response.json(). No SSE, no chunked events, no WebSocket. Request: Content-Type application/json, body exactly {message: string, eventId: string | null} (ChatPanel sends eventId: activeEventId which may be null). Auth: next-auth session cookie via getServerSession(authOptions); no token in payload. Responses: 401 {error:'Please sign in to use Eventiq', content:'Please sign in with Google to start planning your event.'}; 400 {error:'Message is required'}; 500 {error:'Internal server error', content:'Sorry, something went wrong. Please try again.'}; 200 OrchestrateResult: {content: string, eventId?: string, metadata?: {agentName:'Eventiq', creditsCost?: number, toolsUsed?: string[], options?: OptionCard[], thinking?: ThinkingStep[], error?: string}}. options/thinking are omitted (undefined) when empty. Out-of-credits is HTTP 200 with content "You're out of credits..." and metadata {agentName:'Eventiq'} — not an error status. ChatPanel parsing: const data = await response.json(); if (response.status===401) signIn('google'); adopt data.eventId if no activeEventId (setActiveEvent + fetchEvents); then addMessage({id: crypto.randomUUID(), role:'assistant', content: data.content || data.error || 'No response', timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), metadata: data.metadata}). The 'thinking' steps are NOT real-time: server derives them post-hoc from toolsUsed (buildThinkingSteps in orchestrator/index.ts). The cycling loading text ('🔍 Analyzing your request...', '🧠 Orchestrator is deciding...', etc.) is FAKE, client-side setInterval(4000ms) writing chat-store.loadingStatus, started before fetch and cleared in finally. options are regex-parsed server-side from response markdown (parseOptions) only when toolsUsed includes search_venues/search_vendors/search_catering/web_search. Approval flow: ApprovalCard renders from msg.metadata.approvalRequest (ApprovalRequest type); clicking Approve/Reject calls chat-store updateApproval(id, 'approved'|'rejected') optimistically then POST /api/events/approve {approvalId: string, decision: 'approve'|'reject'} -> 200 {success:true, decision} | 400 {error:'Invalid request'} | 404 {error:'Approval not found or already resolved'}; server resolveApproval() resolves an in-memory Map promise (src/agents/orchestrator/approval.ts) — note the current orchestrate() never attaches approvalRequest metadata, so this path is wired but dormant. History: messages loaded via GET /api/events/[id]/messages -> {messages: ChatMessage[]} (oldest-first, Limit 50).
eventDetailsSchema: EventDetails (src/types/event.ts): {confirmedDate?: string; confirmedTime?: string; confirmedVenue?: {name: string; url?: string; price?: string; status?: 'confirmed'|'discussing'|'pending'}; confirmedCatering?: {name: string; url?: string; price?: string; status?: 'confirmed'|'discussing'|'pending'}; schedule?: Array<{time: string; title: string; speaker?: string; status?: 'confirmed'|'discussing'|'pending'}>; contacts?: Array<{name?: string; phone?: string; email?: string; status?: 'confirmed'|'pending'|'messaging'}>; topics?: string[]; budget?: {total?: number; committed?: number; items?: Array<{name: string; amount: number; status: string}>}; visibleSections?: string[]}. WRITERS: (1) Whiteboard agent (src/agents/whiteboard/index.ts) save_event_state tool — the REAL writer; orchestrator delegates with task prefixed "eventId:<id> | ". Field mapping: 'date'->confirmedDate, 'time'->confirmedTime, 'venue'->confirmedVenue (string becomes {name, status:'confirmed'}; object gets status default 'confirmed'), 'catering'->confirmedCatering (same), 'schedule'->details.schedule (array-wrapped), 'contacts'->details.contacts, 'topics'->details.topics, 'budget'->details.budget (non-object becomes {total: value}), 'visibleSections'->details.visibleSections, 'attendees' (number)->updates TOP-LEVEL event.attendeeCount, not details. Every save also appends the field name to visibleSections. remove_event_state deletes the field and filters visibleSections. Persists via updateEvent(eventId, {details}). (2) Orchestrator fallback persistEventDetails (src/agents/orchestrator/index.ts) runs after EVERY chat turn, regex-scraping the assistant response: weekday+month date -> confirmedDate (only if unset); "venue|location|place is|: X" -> confirmedVenue {name} (only if unset); "catering|food|menu is|: X" -> confirmedCatering {name}; "N people|attendees|pax|guests" -> top-level attendeeCount; "topics|agenda include|:" block -> topics. (3) src/agents/graph/tools.ts save_event_details tool is a NO-OP STUB — returns {saved:true, field, value, details, timestamp} but persists NOTHING (comment: "In production this persists to DynamoDB"). graph/tools.ts otherwise only exposes delegate_to_agent and get_current_datetime. CONSUMPTION: GET /api/events/[id]/details returns {details: EventDetails, name, status, attendeeCount, date}. WhiteboardView gates sections on presence: DateSection(date=confirmedDate, time=confirmedTime), VenueSection(venue=confirmedVenue), CateringSection(catering=confirmedCatering), AttendeesSection(contacts, totalCount=attendeeCount||contacts.length), ScheduleSection(items=schedule, table Time/Title/Speaker/Status), BudgetSection(budget: total/committed/remaining/percent bar + items list), TopicsSection(topics chips). visibleSections is written but NEVER read by the UI.

apiRoutes.shape: Auth: getServerSession (session.user.email required) else 401 {error, content}. Req: {message: string, eventId: string|null}. 200: {content: string, eventId?: string, metadata?: {agentName, creditsCost, toolsUsed: string[], options?: OptionCard[], thinking?: ThinkingStep[], error?}}. 400 {error:'Message is required'}; 500 {error, content}. Synchronous JSON, can take tens of seconds (agent graph, Vercel maxDuration).

Auth required else 401 {error:'Unauthorized'}. 200: {events: EventSummary[]} where EventSummary={id, name, date, status:'draft'|'planning'|'confirmed'|'in-progress'|'completed', lastActivity (=updatedAt), pinned: boolean, summary?: '<type> • <attendeeCount> pax'}. Sorted pinned-first then lastActivity desc. NO POST — events are created only by /api/chat.

Auth + ownership (event.userId === session.user.email) else 404 {error:'Not found'}. DELETE -> {success:true}. PATCH body: any subset of allowed fields ['name','pinned','status','date','attendeeCount','type','location','details']; unknown-only body -> 400 {error:'No valid fields to update'}; success -> {success:true}. UI uses PATCH only for {pinned: boolean}.
Auth + ownership else 404. 200: {details: EventDetails (or {}), name: string, status: string, attendeeCount: number, date: string}. Polled every 5s by WhiteboardView.

Auth + ownership else 404. 200: {messages: ChatMessage[]} — ChatMessage={id, role:'user'|'assistant'|'system', content, timestamp (ISO), metadata?: {agentName?, creditsCost?, toolsUsed?, ...}}. DynamoDB query oldest-first with Limit 50 (returns FIRST 50 messages, not most recent).

Auth required. Req: {approvalId: string, decision: 'approve'|'reject'}. 200 {success:true, decision}; 400 {error:'Invalid request'}; 404 {error:'Approval not found or already resolved'}. Resolves against an IN-MEMORY Map (lost on serverless cold start).

Auth required else 401. 200: CreditBalance {userId, balance: number, totalPurchased: number, totalUsed: number, lastUpdated: string}. UI reads .balance only. Auto-initializes a zero record for new users.

Auth required. Req: {tier: '5'|'10'|'20'|'50'} (string keys; $5=500cr, $10=1100, $20=2500, $50=7000). Invalid -> 400 {error:'Invalid tier'}. 200: {url: string} — Stripe Checkout session URL (mode:'payment', USD, metadata {type:'credits', userId: email, credits}); success_url /settings?purchased=true, cancel_url /settings. Client does window.location.href = data.url.

Auth required else 401. GET -> {settings: {customEmail: string, customWhatsApp: string, useCustomEmail: boolean, useCustomWhatsApp: boolean} | null}. POST body = that settings object verbatim -> {success:true}. Stored in credits table under SK 'SETTINGS'.
Standard NextAuth handler from authOptions (src/lib/auth.ts). Google OAuth + dev-only CredentialsProvider; JWT sessions.
Exist but outside the main UI surface: Stripe webhook credits fulfillment, and public form-page submissions (src/app/p/[pageId]).

stores: Both zustand, no persist middleware (state lost on reload). app-store (src/stores/app-store.ts): mode: 'chat'|'whiteboard' (exported type ViewMode, default 'chat'), setMode — used by ModeToggle and page.tsx to switch ChatPanel/WhiteboardView. events: EventSummary[], activeEventId: string|null, eventsLoading: boolean, setEvents, setActiveEvent(eventId|null), fetchEvents() -> GET /api/events sets events, deleteEvent(id) -> DELETE /api/events/[id] then filters locally and nulls activeEventId if it matched, pinEvent(id) -> optimistic toggle + re-sort (pinned first, then lastActivity desc) then PATCH {pinned}. credits: CreditBalance|null + setCredits — DEAD: never populated (CreditsDisplay uses its own useState). isConnected/setConnected — DEAD/unused. Callers: EventSidebar (fetchEvents on session, setActiveEvent, deleteEvent, pinEvent), ChatPanel + WhiteboardInput (activeEventId, fetchEvents, setActiveEvent after event creation), WhiteboardView (activeEventId), ModeToggle (mode/setMode), page.tsx (mode). chat-store (src/stores/chat-store.ts): messages: ChatMessage[], isLoading, historyLoading, loadingStatus: string, pendingApprovals: ApprovalRequest[]. addMessage (append), setMessages (replace), setLoading, setLoadingStatus, addApproval (sorted insert — NEVER called anywhere), updateApproval(id, status) (called by ApprovalCard for optimistic resolve), clearMessages (clears messages + pendingApprovals), loadConversation(eventId) (sets historyLoading + clears messages, GET /api/events/[id]/messages, setMessages). Callers: ChatPanel (everything except approvals; uses getState() imperatively inside handlers), EventSidebar (clearMessages on New Event, loadConversation on event click), WhiteboardInput (addMessage/setLoading — shares the same message list so chat and whiteboard stay in sync), ApprovalCard (updateApproval). pendingApprovals is destructured in ChatPanel but never rendered.
authFlow: next-auth (pages router style authOptions in src/lib/auth.ts, handler at /api/auth/[...nextauth]). Providers: GoogleProvider (GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET) + a dev-only CredentialsProvider ('Dev Login', email field) when NODE_ENV==='development'. session.strategy='jwt'; jwt callback copies user.email to token; session callback sets (session.user as any).id = token.email — userId throughout the app IS the email (session.user.email), used as DynamoDB key (USER#<email>) and event.userId. signIn callback grants 1000 welcome credits via addCredits(email, 1000, 'welcome-bonus') when totalPurchased===0 && balance===0 (non-blocking on failure). Client: layout.tsx wraps everything in SessionProvider (thin wrapper over next-auth/react SessionProvider) + sonner Toaster. page.tsx itself does NOT gate on session — the shell always renders. Gating is per-component: ChatPanel: empty state shows 'Sign in with Google to get started' button when !session && status!=='loading'; submit with no session calls signIn('google'); send button shows Lock icon when signed out; input placeholder changes; 401 from /api/chat triggers signIn('google'). EventSidebar: only fetches events when session?.user?.email. UserMenu (bottom of sidebar): status==='loading' -> skeleton; no session -> 'Sign in / with Google' button calling signIn('google'); signed in -> avatar (image or initial), name, email, click opens upward dropdown with: Settings (href /settings), Credits (href /settings#credits), Sign out (signOut()), plus a fixed-inset backdrop to close. Settings page: renders 'Please sign in to access settings.' when !session. All API routes use getServerSession(authOptions) and 401 without session; event routes additionally 404 when event.userId !== session.user.email.
creditsFlow: Balance fetch: GET /api/credits returns full CreditBalance {userId, balance, totalPurchased, totalUsed, lastUpdated}; both CreditsDisplay and Settings read only d.balance. CreditsDisplay (src/components/layout/CreditsDisplay.tsx): fetches once when session?.user?.email appears, local useState (NOT app-store.credits), renders null until loaded, then an absolutely-positioned pill (top-4 left-4, z-10) 'N credits' with Sparkles icon. IMPORTANT: it is imported in src/app/page.tsx but NOT rendered anywhere in the current JSX tree — currently dead UI. It never refreshes after chat spends credits. Purchase flow: Settings page (/settings) shows 4 tiers (string keys '5','10','20','50' -> $5/500, $10/1,100, $20/2,500, $50/7,000 credits); handlePurchase POSTs /api/credits/checkout {tier} -> {url} -> window.location.href = url (Stripe-hosted Checkout, USD, one-time payment, metadata {type:'credits', userId: email, credits}); Stripe redirects to /settings?purchased=true (success banner via searchParams) or /settings (cancel); fulfillment happens via /api/webhooks/stripe -> addCredits. Spending: orchestrate() pre-checks getCreditBalance(userId); balance <= 0 returns the 200 'out of credits' message; otherwise deductCredits(userId, 2, 'agent_call', eventId) up front + deductCredits(userId, toolsUsed.length, 'tool_calls', eventId) after the graph; total surfaced as metadata.creditsCost on the assistant message and displayed next to agentName in the chat bubble. New users get 1000 credits at first sign-in. Note: CREDIT_PACKAGES in src/types/payment.ts ('credits-50' etc.) is stale and unused by the checkout route.
whiteboardRole: WhiteboardView (src/components/whiteboard/WhiteboardView.tsx) is the read-only-ish 'decisions board' for the active event. Data source: GET /api/events/[activeEventId]/details -> {details, name, status, attendeeCount, date}, fetched on mount/activeEventId change AND POLLED every 5 seconds via setInterval — this polling is the ONLY mechanism by which the UI learns the agent updated EventDetails (chat mode never refetches details; switching modes triggers a fresh fetch because WhiteboardView remounts). Renders: header bar with event name, raw date string, StatusBadge (draft/planning/confirmed/in-progress/completed); empty states ('No Active Event' when no activeEventId; 'No confirmed details yet' when no sections); then a presence-gated layout: top grid (1/2/4 cols) of DateSection (confirmedDate/confirmedTime), VenueSection (confirmedVenue: name, price, url 'View' link, status pill with border color), CateringSection (same shape), AttendeesSection (contacts list ≤6 shown with ✓/📱/⏳ icons, confirmed/pending counts, total pill); full-width ScheduleSection (table: Time/Title/Speaker/Status pill per item); bottom 2-col grid of BudgetSection (total/committed/remaining, percent progress bar color-coded >90% red >70% yellow, itemized list) and TopicsSection (chip per topic). At the bottom WhiteboardInput — a second chat input that POSTs the exact same /api/chat protocol ({message, eventId}) and writes user+assistant messages into the SHARED chat-store, including the eventId-adoption logic, so the conversation stays consistent when toggling modes. ModeToggle (src/components/layout/ModeToggle.tsx): two-button segmented control (Chat=MessageSquare, Whiteboard=LayoutGrid) bound to app-store mode/setMode with a framer-motion layoutId='mode-indicator' sliding background; page.tsx renders it in a top bar and swaps ChatPanel vs WhiteboardView on mode.
existingChatFeatures:
Markdown rendering (MarkdownMessage): react-markdown with custom components (h1->h3 ... downscaled headings, lists, code, hr); links rendered as plain bold text (URL hidden) plus a collapsible 'Sources (n)' footer extracting markdown + bare URLs into domain chips that open in new tabs; prose-invert for user variant (user messages actually render as plain

, not markdown)

Thinking trace (ThinkingTrace): collapsible 'Show/Hide thinking • N steps' panel from msg.metadata.thinking (ThinkingStep {tool, query?, source?, url?, status:'completed'|'failed'}); TOOL_LABELS maps tool names (search_venues->'Searched venues', send_whatsapp->'Sent WhatsApp message', etc.); green check / red X per step; query in mono font, 'via <source>', external link. Steps are synthesized server-side from toolsUsed — not real chain-of-thought

Legacy reasoning trace (ReasoningTrace): collapsible panel from msg.metadata.reasoningTrace (ReasoningStep {stepId, agentName, action, rationale, dataSources[], status, duration?}); header shows last step's agentName+action with aggregate spinner/check/X. Currently never populated by the orchestrator but still wired in ChatPanel

Approval cards (ApprovalCard): from msg.metadata.approvalRequest (ApprovalRequest {id, actionType:'payment'|'communication'|'booking'|'checkout', amount?, currency?, recipient?, description, consequenceApprove, consequenceReject, status, createdAt}); pending -> amber warning card with formatted currency (default SGD), recipient, approve/reject consequence lines, Approve/Reject buttons -> optimistic chat-store.updateApproval + POST /api/events/approve {approvalId, decision}; resolved -> compact '✓ Approved'/'✗ Rejected' line. Dormant: orchestrator never emits approvalRequest today

Option cards (OptionCardCarousel): from msg.metadata.options (OptionCard {type, name, price?, description, url?, imageUrl?, location?, score?, category?}); numbered cards with type badge color map (venue blue, vendor purple, catering/food orange, result emerald, option gray), star score, price, 'Website' link, hover check-button; clicking pre-fills the input with 'I'd like to go with "<name>" (<price>). Please confirm this choice.' (NO auto-submit); 'Search for more →' shuffle pre-fills 'Can you search for more options?...'. Options are regex-parsed server-side and only when a search tool ran

Action items (ActionItems): rendered above the input when messages exist; regex-parses the LAST assistant message for a 'next steps/questions' heading followed by numbered/bullet items, else trailing '?' lines; max 4 chips with keyword-based emoji (🍽️📍⏰📞💰👥📌); hidden if the latest message is from the user; clicking fills the input

Message actions (MessageActions): hover toolbar per non-system message — Copy (clipboard + 2s check) for all; Edit for user messages (enters inline edit mode... handleStartEdit/handleEditSubmit truncate the local message list after the edited message and re-POST /api/chat — CLIENT-side truncation only, server history untouched); ThumbsUp/ThumbsDown for assistant (purely local state, never sent to any API); Retry for assistant (pre-fills input with the preceding user message)

Loading states: isLoading bubble with spinner + fake cycling status text (5 messages rotated every 4s via chat-store.loadingStatus, started before fetch, cleared in finally); historyLoading full-pane spinner 'Loading conversation...' when switching events; sidebar SkeletonEvent shimmer rows while eventsLoading
Message metadata header: agentName + '• N credits' (metadata.creditsCost) shown above assistant content

History loading: ChatPanel useEffect fetches /api/events/[activeEventId]/messages on activeEventId/session change (only replaces store when messages.length > 0); EventSidebar click also calls chat-store.loadConversation(eventId) — double fetch by design
Auth-aware input: signed-out shows sign-in CTA in empty state, Lock icon instead of Send, placeholder 'Sign in to start planning...'; submit and 401 responses trigger signIn('google')
Empty state: logo, 'Eventiq' headline, Singapore-focused description
Auto-scroll to bottom on every messages change (messagesEndRef.scrollIntoView smooth)
Paperclip attach button exists but is a no-op (no handler); FileAttachment type exists but unused
System/error messages: role 'system' rendered in destructive style; client fetch failure adds a local system message 'Failed to process your message.'
risks: No streaming: /api/chat is one long blocking JSON call (multi-agent graph can run 30-60s). A new UI assuming SSE/chunks will break; it must keep a single fetch with generous timeout and reproduce (or replace) the fake client-side status cycling, and must clear the interval in finally or loadingStatus leaks globally

Implicit event creation: there is NO POST /api/events. New UI must send eventId:null on first message and MUST adopt response.eventId (setActiveEvent + fetchEvents) — forgetting this silently creates a duplicate event per message and orphans conversation history

Whiteboard freshness = 5s polling of GET /api/events/[id]/details. Removing the poll silently freezes the board; chat mode has no details refresh at all, so a unified UI that doesn't remount/poll will show stale agent updates

metadata.options and metadata.thinking are NOT persisted (createMessage saves only {agentName, creditsCost, toolsUsed}) — option cards and thinking traces vanish on reload/event-switch. A new UI must not assume they exist in history; conversely don't 'fix' by persisting without checking DynamoDB item size

Approval system is in-memory (Map in src/agents/orchestrator/approval.ts) — lost on serverless cold start; POST /api/events/approve can 404 legitimately. Also nothing currently creates approvals, but ApprovalCard + /api/events/approve must stay wired or future agent flows break

Double message fetch on event switch: ChatPanel's useEffect AND EventSidebar's loadConversation both hit /api/events/[id]/messages. ChatPanel's version only calls setMessages when messages.length > 0, so it relies on loadConversation/clearMessages to empty the pane — replacing only one path can leave stale messages from the previous event

GET /api/events/[id]/messages returns the OLDEST 50 messages (ScanIndexForward true + Limit 50), not the most recent; long conversations truncate at the tail. Don't assume recency or add pagination without changing the query

Out-of-credits and agent errors return HTTP 200 with friendly content (metadata.error for agent failures) — a new UI keying error states off HTTP status will mislabel these as successful replies; conversely 401/500 bodies include both error and content and ChatPanel falls back data.content || data.error
userId IS the email (session.user.email) across DB keys and ownership checks; ownership failure returns 404 not 403. Any UI auth change must preserve session.user.email

Edit-message truncation is client-only: server conversation (last 10 messages fed to the agent) still contains the 'deleted' turns, so agent context diverges from what the user sees — preserve this quirk knowingly or fix server-side, but don't assume parity

Option cards / event details extraction are brittle regexes over the assistant's markdown (parseOptions, persistEventDetails, ActionItems). UI features that alter what content gets sent back, or any prompt to change response formatting, will silently change which options/details/next-step chips appear

Whiteboard agent requires the 'eventId:<id> | ' task prefix and is the only real EventDetails writer; save_event_details in src/agents/graph/tools.ts is a NO-OP stub — do not assume calling it persists anything
WhiteboardInput shares the chat-store message list with ChatPanel; splitting them into separate stores desyncs conversation between modes

PATCH /api/events/[id] only accepts ['name','pinned','status','date','attendeeCount','type','location','details']; sending other fields alone yields 400. pinEvent does optimistic local re-sort that must match the server's sort (pinned first, lastActivity desc) or the sidebar flickers
Credits tier keys are magic strings '5'|'10'|'20'|'50' duplicated in settings page and checkout route; CREDIT_PACKAGES in types/payment.ts is stale/unused — don't 'unify' onto it

CreditsDisplay is imported in page.tsx but NOT rendered; app-store.credits/setCredits and isConnected/setConnected are dead. chat-store.addApproval and pendingApprovals are never used in rendering. Removing them is safe-ish, but re-rendering CreditsDisplay note its absolute top-left positioning and that it never refreshes after spending

Zustand stores have no persistence: reload loses activeEventId, mode, and messages; current UX recovers via fetchEvents + user re-clicking an event — a new UI adding persistence must still call loadConversation
visibleSections is maintained by the whiteboard agent but ignored by WhiteboardView (presence-based gating). Gating new UI sections on visibleSections would hide data the current UI shows
Settings GET can return {settings: null}; the page only overwrites local state when d.settings is truthy — a new settings UI must handle null and POST the full 4-field object verbatim
Dev CredentialsProvider only exists when NODE_ENV==='development'; e2e/new-UI testing against production builds must use Google OAuth
